chapter: Chapter 3: Electricity: Circuits and their Components

questions.question: What is an electric circuit?
questions.answer: An electric circuit is a closed path through which electric current flows from a power source through various components and back to the source.

questions.question: Explain the difference between an open circuit and a closed circuit.
questions.answer: A closed circuit allows electric current to flow because the path is complete, while an open circuit has a break in the path and therefore current cannot flow.

questions.question: What role does a battery play in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: A battery provides the electrical energy needed to push electrons through the circuit, enabling current to flow.

questions.question: What is an electric current?
questions.answer: Electric current is the flow of electric charges, usually electrons, through a conductor.

questions.question: Name two materials that are good conductors of electricity and explain why.
questions.answer: Copper and aluminum are good conductors because they allow electric charges to move through them easily.

questions.question: What are insulators? Give two examples.
questions.answer: Insulators are materials that do not allow electric current to pass through easily. Examples include rubber and plastic.

questions.question: What is the function of a switch in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: A switch is used to open or close a circuit, thereby controlling the flow of electric current.

questions.question: Explain what a resistor does in an electric circuit.
questions.answer: A resistor limits or controls the amount of current flowing through a circuit.

questions.question: What is meant by resistance?
questions.answer: Resistance is the opposition offered by a material to the flow of electric current.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure electric current.
questions.answer: An ammeter is used to measure electric current in a circuit.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure voltage.
questions.answer: A voltmeter is used to measure the potential difference (voltage) between two points in a circuit.

questions.question: What is meant by potential difference?
questions.answer: Potential difference is the work done in moving a unit charge from one point to another in a circuit.

questions.question: What is Ohm’s Law?
questions.answer: Ohm’s Law states that the current flowing through a conductor is directly proportional to the voltage across it, provided temperature remains constant.

questions.question: What is a series circuit?
questions.answer: A series circuit is a circuit in which components are connected one after another so that current flows through each component in the same path.

questions.question: What is a parallel circuit?
questions.answer: A parallel circuit is a circuit where components are connected in separate branches so that current has multiple paths to flow.

questions.question: Why are household appliances connected in parallel circuits?
questions.answer: Household appliances are connected in parallel so that each appliance receives the same voltage and can operate independently.

questions.question: What is a fuse and what is its purpose?
questions.answer: A fuse is a safety device that melts and breaks the circuit if excessive current flows, protecting electrical appliances.

questions.question: Explain the concept of short circuit.
questions.answer: A short circuit occurs when current flows through an unintended path with very low resistance, causing excessive current flow.

questions.question: What are circuit diagrams?
questions.answer: Circuit diagrams are graphical representations of electrical circuits using standardized symbols.

questions.question: Why are standard symbols used in circuit diagrams?
questions.answer: Standard symbols make circuit diagrams easier to read and understand universally.

questions.question: What is meant by electric power?
questions.answer: Electric power is the rate at which electrical energy is consumed or produced in a circuit.

questions.question: Explain why copper wires are commonly used in electrical circuits.
questions.answer: Copper is used because it has low resistance and allows electric current to flow easily.

questions.question: What is a load in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: A load is a component that uses electrical energy to perform a function, such as a bulb, fan, or heater.

questions.question: Explain how a bulb glows in a circuit.
questions.answer: When current flows through the filament of a bulb, it heats up and emits light due to high temperature.

questions.question: Why is insulation necessary in electrical wires?
questions.answer: Insulation prevents electric shocks and stops current from leaking out of the wire.
